schema_version: v0 — skill-charter shape; migrates into the intention envelope when MAP.md Q6 lands
what: Interim checkpoint: stage, commit, and push the current work with a clear message. A non-terminal marker in the fluid middle, not a claim of doneness.
why: Work in the fluid middle needs recoverable checkpoints — a place to return to — that carry no false weight of completion. This is deliberately distinct from the durability-of-stones GATE (every stone committed + pushed), which belongs on the emit chokepoint as physics, not on a skill someone fires. A skill invented to force a behavior is policy standing in for a missing gate; keeping the two apart is what lets the checkpoint stay a light, honest act.
state: v0 — first cast 2026-07-15. The durability gate remains an IOU (CLAUDE.md 'rules awaiting physics').
distinction: interim-checkpoint SKILL vs durability-of-stones GATE — they coexist; collide only under an exclusive-and-terminal reading, which the learning-loop keystone forbids (see memory ask-the-why-to-distinguish).
how_it_learns: It doesn't, because mechanical: /commit is an interim checkpoint with no judgment to refine; the learning lives in the durability GATE it is distinct from, not here. A valid 'doesn't learn, because X' answer.
traces_to: Law 4 (durability is a gate awaiting physics, tracked as an IOU)
owner: the skills charter gates writes; Akien ratifies changes (signature gate)
guardrail: commit/push only when Akien has asked; end messages with the Co-Authored-By line; branch before committing durable work to a default branch.
proof: skill node → done only when cairnmap recompiles green (derivation gate). IOU until cairnmap exists — tracked in proofs/.
provenance: reclassified in the founding design conversation from 'a skill to force commits' to 'an interim-checkpoint utility'; the forcing role moved to a gate (MAP.md 'Gates, not supervisors', 2026-07-15).
